title: What Gen Z Really Wants From Work
subtitle: Z世代为什么不像前辈那样拼命工作？

paragraphs.en: As Generation Z enters the job market, employers are eager to understand how this cohort differs from millennials. Even in a relatively strong hiring cycle, many Gen Z graduates approach careers with caution. They grew up with memories of financial shocks and household insecurity.
paragraphs.zh: 随着Z世代进入就业市场，雇主们急于了解这一代人与千禧一代有何不同。即便在招聘需求相对旺盛的周期里，许多Z世代毕业生仍以谨慎的态度看待职业选择。他们成长于金融动荡与家庭不安全感交织的记忆之中。

paragraphs.en: Career surveys show they often choose majors with employability in mind and rank job security very highly. Training opportunities and work-life balance also score above vague mission language in many studies. Student debt pressure further strengthens their preference for predictable income.
paragraphs.zh: 职业调查显示，他们选择专业时往往以就业前景为导向，并将工作稳定性置于极高位置。在许多研究中，培训机会和工作生活平衡的得分也高于模糊的使命宣言。学生债务的压力进一步强化了他们对可预期收入的偏好。

words.meaning: n. 可就业能力，受雇资格
adj. 可预测的，可预见的

paragraphs.en: This marks a shift from the flexibility-first story often linked with millennials. In a gig-heavy economy, certainty can feel like a premium benefit rather than a baseline right. For employers, credible long-term development pathways may now matter more than branding slogans.
paragraphs.zh: 这与常被归于千禧一代的“优先弹性”叙事形成了鲜明对比。在一个零工经济占比上升的社会里，确定性更像是一种稀缺福利，而非默认权利。对雇主而言，可信的长期发展路径如今可能比品牌口号更为重要。
